Before Using
Health and Safety Information
Manual
Health and Safety Information
Please read this electronic manual carefully before using the software to ensure proper use.

Please read the Health and Safety Information on the Wii U Menu before using this software. It contains important details regarding your health and safety. Parents or guardians should read and explain this information to small children.
©2012 Nintendo
Close Manual
Table of Contents
What is Nintendo eShop?
Manual
What is Nintendo eShop?

Nintendo eShop is an online store where you can purchase downloadable Wii U software, add-on content, and more. You can also view detailed information and videos of software, or download demo versions.
※To purchase software, you must first add funds to your balance. 8
※If you register the same Nintendo Network ID on both a Wii U console and a Nintendo 3DS system, you can share your balance and account activity.
Purchasable Software
●Wii U Download Software
This includes download-only software for Wii U and download versions of packaged Wii U software.
●Virtual Console
These are classic games originally released on older consoles such as NES and Super NES.
Virtual Console Special Offer

If you already own the Wii version of a Virtual Console title (either by transferring it from a Wii console, or by purchasing it on the Wii Shop Channel on the Wii Menu), you can purchase the Wii U version of the same title at a special discount price. Please visit the official Nintendo website for more details.

※If you want to restrict access for children, you can configure Parental Controls from the Wii U Menu. You can view the Wii U Electronic Manual by pressing the HOME Button on the Wii U Menu and selecting the Manual icon .
Restricted Item
Restriction Details
Shopping Services
You can restrict the purchase of software and the use of credit cards or electronic money.
※A loading icon will be displayed when loading or saving data. You cannot open the HOME Button Menu while saving.
※Screenshots shown in this manual are examples. Actual screens may vary.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Main Screen Layout
Manual
Main Screen Layout
Operate by touching the screen of the Wii U GamePad. Slide your finger up and down to scroll, and touch icons to select them.
1 Menu Bar
Returns to the home screen of Nintendo eShop.
View various account activity details and configure settings. 4
Displays your current balance. Touch to add funds to your balance. 8
Search for software by keyword, category, genre, or other criteria. 5
Goes back to the previous screen.
※On the home screen, "Close" is displayed. Touch to close Nintendo eShop.
2 Search 5
3Enter Download Code / Charts / News
●Enter Download Code　11
Enter a download code to download software.
●Charts　12
View charts of best-selling software, user-recommended titles, or popular videos.
●News
Read the latest news from Nintendo eShop.
4 Various Icons
Displays lists of software, feature sheets, and videos.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
My Menu
Manual
My Menu
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Touch "My Menu" on the menu bar to view details or configure settings.
●Help
Manual
Opens this electronic manual.
About Nintendo eShop
Provides information about Nintendo eShop services.
Support / Contact Information
Displays contact information for customer support. Please check the details before contacting us.
●Adding Funds8
Wish List
Displays titles added to your Wish List.
Touch a software icon to go to its details page. 6
Touch the "View List" button to display all titles registered on your Wish List.
※You can add up to 200 titles to your Wish List.
User Recommendations
Displays titles you can recommend.
Touch a software icon and answer three simple questions to recommend a title you've played to others.
Touch the "View List" button to display all titles you can recommend.
The voting results are compiled and displayed on software details pages and in the charts. 6 12
※Each user can recommend a title only once, and only after playing it for at least one hour.
※You cannot recommend a title if your age is lower than the software rating.
※Some software cannot be recommended.
※You can view your recommendation history in your Account Activity.
History
●Redownloadable Software
View and redownload previously purchased software or add-on content.
●Account Activity
Displays records of purchased software and added funds. Touch "View Details" to view receipts.

※If you register the same Nintendo Network ID on both a Wii U console and a Nintendo 3DS system, your account activity will be shared. However, Nintendo 3DS activity from before the ID was registered will not be displayed on Wii U.
Settings
●Notification Settings
Choose whether to receive notifications from Nintendo eShop on your Wii U Menu.
●Location Settings
Register, modify, or delete your location information.
※Location information is used to calculate tax when purchasing software.
※The configured location information applies only to Nintendo eShop.
●Language Settings
Change the display language for Nintendo eShop. You must close and restart Nintendo eShop for the language change to take effect.
※The language setting applies only to Nintendo eShop.
Searching for Software
Manual
Searching for Software
There are three ways to search for software:
1Search by Keyword
Enter the title of the software or other keywords to search.
2Search by Criteria
Search by configuring filters such as category, genre, or keyword.
3Search by Feature Banners
Select feature banners or software categories on the home screen.
Search Results
Software and videos are displayed in chronological order. Touch a title to open its details page, or touch "Wish List" to add it to your Wish List. 4
1You can sort results by release date, user rating, or name.
2The following icons indicate content available for download on the software details page:
Demo
Add-on Content
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Viewing Software Details
Manual
Viewing Software Details
Touch "View Details" to open the software details screen.
You can view detailed software information, purchase software, or add items to your Wish List. Demos, add-on content, or passes may also be available depending on the software.
※Available details and options vary by software.
●Proceed to Purchase Screen9
●Add to Wish List
Touch to add the title to your Wish List. 4
●Demos

Download demo versions of software for free.

※Demos may have restrictions on play time or launch counts. Deleting and redownloading the demo will not reset these limits.

※Limits are shared among all users on the console.
●Add-on Content10
●Passes10
About User Ratings
The User Rating displayed on the details page shows the average recommendation score in half-star increments. 4
※Ratings are not displayed for unreleased software.

※See "About Data Collection" for details on recommendation statistics.
14
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Watching Videos
Manual
Watching Videos
Select videos on the software details page or other screens to watch clips related to the software.
1Pause / Play Button
Touch to pause video playback. Touch again to resume.
2Playback Bar
Displays the current playback position. Slide to rewind or fast forward.
3Fit Screen Button
Touch to fit the video to the height or width of your TV screen. Touch again to revert.
4Options
Displays the video title and related clips. You can change video quality or touch "View Details" to go to the software details page.
※Depending on your internet connection, video or audio playback may stutter.
Operating with Buttons and Control Sticks
You can also control video playback using buttons and control sticks on the Wii U GamePad.
Show/Hide Toolbar
Play/Pause Video
Fast Forward / Rewind
Skip Forward 15s
Double Speed Playback
Hold
Skip Backward 10s
Exit Playback
※Videos cannot be saved to the console's system memory, USB storage devices, or SD cards.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Adding Funds
Manual
Adding Funds
Touch "Add Funds" on the menu bar to open the fund adding screen.
※Funds are managed separately for each user.
To add funds (charge your balance), you need one of the following:
Nintendo Prepaid Card
Nintendo Prepaid Code
Credit Card
※Prepaid cards and added funds cannot be used in the Wii Shop Channel.
※If you registered the same Nintendo Network ID on both your Wii U console and Nintendo 3DS system, your balance will be shared with the Nintendo 3DS eShop.
Add Funds using a Nintendo Prepaid Code
Select this option to use a Nintendo Prepaid Card or Prepaid Code. You can also view details on how to purchase prepaid codes.
Add Funds using a Credit Card

Select this option to use a credit card. Receipts can be viewed in your Account Activity on My Menu. 4
You can save credit card details to add funds in the future simply by entering your password.
※Your saved credit card details will be deleted if you enter the password incorrectly three times.
※If you forget your password, delete the credit card information and register it again.
Confirm Credit Card Info
Verify or delete saved credit card information.
※Users under 18 cannot use credit cards unless parents or guardians give consent. Please follow the on-screen instructions.
Payment Services Act Notices
View notices based on the Payment Services Act.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Prepaid Electronic Money (Suica, PASMO, etc.)
Use Electronic Money

Select this option to add funds using prepaid electronic money card. Receipts can be viewed in Account Activity. 4 Follow the on-screen instructions and touch your card to the NFC area mark on the Wii U GamePad.
Supported electronic money cards (as of July 22, 2014):
※Touch the card firmly to the NFC area mark as guided. If the card is too far or touched too briefly, it might not be read correctly.
Purchasing Software
Manual
Purchasing Software
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Touch "Proceed to Purchase Screen" on the details page 6 or search results 5 to proceed. Follow the on-screen prompts to complete the purchase.
※Software download starts automatically after purchase. Check progress in Download Management on the HOME Button Menu.
※Purchased software can be played by all users registered on the console.
Purchasing Add-on Content / Passes
Manual
Purchasing Add-on Content / Passes
Select and purchase "Add-on Content" or "Passes" from the software details page 9.
Purchasing Add-on Content
Available add-on content can be purchased if it has been released.
●Search Content
Search and filter available add-on content by keyword, genre, or price.
●Add-on Content List
Touch to open details for specific add-on content.
●Checkboxes
Touch the checkbox next to the content you want to select it . Touch "View Selected Items" and follow the instructions to purchase.
※Add-on content types and prices vary by software.
※Receipts for add-on content purchases can be viewed in Account Activity on My Menu. 4
※All sales of add-on content are final (no refunds, returns, or exchanges).
※Purchased add-on content can be redownloaded for free even if deleted.
※You cannot redownload content if your Nintendo Network ID is deleted.
※Redownload services may be suspended or terminated without prior notice.
※Purchased add-on content is saved in the same location as the parent software.
※Add-on content is accessible to all users registered on the console.
Purchasing Passes
Passes can be purchased if the software provides subscription-based services.
Passes have an expiration date and take effect immediately upon purchase. If you buy a pass before your current one expires, the new duration will be added to the remaining time.

●Example 1:
When purchasing a 30-Day Pass

●Example 2:
When purchasing a 30-Day Pass before expiration
※Pass durations and prices vary by software.
※Subscriptions do not auto-renew automatically even if you have enough balance. Please purchase a new pass when the current one expires.
※Pass purchase records and start dates can be viewed in Account Activity on My Menu. 4
※All sales of passes are final (no refunds, returns, or exchanges).
※Active passes are accessible to all users registered on the console.
※To buy an additional pass while one is active, you must use the same user account that made the original purchase.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
※Filtering by genre might not be supported for some add-on content.
Entering Download Codes
Manual
Entering Download Codes
Enter a download code to download software.
What is a Download Code?
A 16-character alphanumeric code used to download paid or free software, add-on content, or passes.
How to Obtain Download Codes
You can purchase Download Cards containing a download code at Nintendo retailers or select convenience stores.
You can purchase download codes directly from select online stores.
※Purchase methods vary by retailer. Please check the official Nintendo website for more details.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Viewing Charts
Manual
Viewing Charts
Touch "Charts" on the home screen to view charts of best-selling software, user-recommended titles, or popular videos.
1 Select Chart Type
Best-sellers and popular videos charts are based on activity from the past two weeks. User recommendations charts cover the past 12 weeks.

※See "About Data Collection" for details on chart statistics.
14
※If titles tie, the newer release is listed first.
2 Filter Charts
You can filter charts by software category.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
Q&A
Manual
Q&A
●Software Purchases

Q

How do I pay for my purchases?

A

Payments are deducted from your balance, which can be charged using prepaid cards, credit cards, or electronic money.

Q

How do I use coupons?

A

If you have a coupon code from a promotion, select "Use Coupon" on the screen after touching "Proceed to Purchase Screen" and follow the instructions.

Q

Which prepaid cards and codes can be used to add funds?
A
The following can be used:
Nintendo Prepaid Card
Nintendo Prepaid Code
The following cannot be used:
Wii Point Card
Wii Point Code
Nintendo Point Card
Nintendo Point Code
※See "How to Obtain Download Codes" on the Add Funds screen for more details. 8

Q

Where can I buy Nintendo Prepaid Cards?

A

You can purchase them at Nintendo retailers or select convenience stores.

Q

How do I use a Nintendo Prepaid Card?

A

Scratch off the silver panel on the back of the card and enter the 16-character alphanumeric code. 8

Q

Where can I buy Nintendo Prepaid Codes?

A

You can purchase them at convenience store kiosks or cash registers. Check the official Nintendo website for supported chains.

Q

What credit cards are supported?

A

VISA, MasterCard, and JCB.

Q

Can I buy software using Wii Points?

A

No. Wii Points can only be used in the Wii Shop Channel.

Q

Can I use my Nintendo 3DS eShop balance?

A

Yes, if you register the same Nintendo Network ID on both your Wii U console and Nintendo 3DS system.

Q

Where can find codes issued during a purchase?

A

You can find them on your receipt. Go to My Menu -> Account Activity -> View Details. Look for the "Issue Number" item.

Q

Can I view my Nintendo 3DS account activity?

A

Yes. However, Nintendo 3DS activity from before the ID was registered will not be displayed on Wii U.
●About Software

Q

Are manuals available for software purchased on Nintendo eShop?

A

Yes. You can access the Electronic Manual by pressing the HOME Button while the software is running and selecting "Manual."

Q

Does redownloading software cost money?

A

No, it is free of charge (unless your Nintendo Network ID is deleted). Note that software distribution may be suspended or terminated without notice.
●Data Management

Q

How long does it take to download purchased software?

A

Download times depend on the size of the software and your internet connection speed.
You can check download progress in Download Management on the HOME Button Menu.

Q

Where is purchased software saved?

A

It is saved to the system memory or a USB storage device. It cannot be saved to an SD Card.

Q

Can I copy or move data between system memory and a USB storage device?

A

Yes. See Data Management under System Settings on the Wii U Menu for details.
●Support

Q

Who should I contact for support?

A

It depends on the nature of your inquiry. Go to My Menu -> Help -> Support / Contact Information. 4
Close Manual
Table of Contents

Q

Can I add funds to nfc cards like Suica or PASMO on the Wii U console?

A

No. You cannot add funds to the card itself on the Wii U. Check the electronic money card issuer's website for funding instructions.

Q

Which electronic money cards are supported?
A
The following cards can be used (as of July 22, 2014):
Kitaca
PASMO
Suica
manaca
TOICA
ICOCA
Hayakaken
nimoca
SUGOCA
About Data Collection
Manual
About Data Collection
User Ratings and video view counts are calculated based on specific criteria.
●User Ratings
The score is the average of recommendations, displayed in half-star increments.
※Ratings are reflected starting from the first vote.
※To calculate a more accurate average, the top 5% and bottom 5% of ratings are excluded.
●Software Charts
Software charts aggregate votes and list titles with 20 or more recommendations, sorted by score.
●Video Charts
Video charts aggregate the number of times clips were viewed, sorted by view count.
Update Frequency
Data is compiled and updated every three hours (updates are not reflected immediately).
※Updates may be delayed due to system processing requirements.
Close Manual
Table of Contents
